response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is technically proficient across the required stack — TypeScript, REST APIs, React, Node.js — and brings genuine strengths in clean architecture and AI/LLM tooling. However, he is a Staff Software Engineer at Google with 15+ years of experience spanning embedded systems, high-throughput microservices, and AI-agent infrastructure. Placing him in an entry-level individual contributor role is an extreme seniority mismatch that creates serious retention and motivation risk, and the application raises more questions than it answers about fit and intent.
strengths: Strong TypeScript and full-stack Node.js experience across multiple senior roles (Google, Meta, RIDE Capital) directly matching the core stack
Demonstrated clean-architecture discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and craftsmanship practices explicitly called out
LLM/agentic tooling experience (MCP frameworks at Google) and Kubernetes exposure fulfill the highest-value nice-to-have qualifications
concerns: Severely overqualified: current role is Staff Engineer at Google — applying for a junior/entry-level position is a multi-level seniority mismatch that signals high churn or misalignment risk
No explicit PostgreSQL experience; database exposure leans toward MongoDB and generic SQL, missing the preferred relational stack
No NestJS mentioned anywhere in the resume; backend framework experience is Node.js/Express-style, leaving a gap in the preferred backend framework
